_instructions: Maps ability names/descriptions to PASSIVE/STATE predicates and modifiers

DEFER_TARGET.category: Abilities that apply against specific attack or defense types

hard_target.name: Hard Target
hard_target.description: +2 bonus to Reflex Defense against ranged attacks while moving at least 2 squares on your turn

defensive_stance.name: Defensive Stance
defensive_stance.description: +2 bonus to all defenses while fighting defensively

melee_defense.name: Template: Defense vs Melee
melee_defense.description: Any ability providing defense bonus against melee attacks
melee_defense.NOTE: Use this as template; replace value with actual bonus

ranged_defense.name: Template: Defense vs Ranged
ranged_defense.description: Any ability providing defense bonus against ranged attacks
ranged_defense.NOTE: Use this as template; replace value with actual bonus

DEFER_ACTION.category: Abilities that apply during specific actions or on specific turns

on_your_turn_bonus.name: Template: Bonus on Your Turn
on_your_turn_bonus.description: Any ability providing bonus that applies on character's turn
on_your_turn_bonus.NOTE: Use this as template; adjust target and value

while_moving.name: Template: While Moving
while_moving.description: Any ability providing bonus while moving
while_moving.NOTE: Use this as template; adjust target and value

once_per_round.name: Template: Once Per Round
once_per_round.description: Any ability that can be used once per round
once_per_round.NOTE: Use this as template; requires round tracking in context

for_each_ally.name: Template: For Each Ally Bonus
for_each_ally.description: Bonus scales with number of allies (e.g., +1 per ally within 12 squares)
for_each_ally.NOTE: Use evaluateStatePredicatesNumeric() to get ally count

when_flanked.name: Template: Bonus When Enemy Flanked
when_flanked.description: +2 bonus to attack when target is flanked

DEFER_SUBSTITUTION.category: Abilities that substitute one action for another (CANNOT MIGRATE - requires action economy)
DEFER_SUBSTITUTION.reason: Substitution mechanics require action economy gating, not state predicates
examples: Instead of using skill X, you can use skill Y
You can use ability A in place of ability B
Replaces standard action with full attack

DEFER_TEMPORAL.category: Abilities tied to duration or specific round numbers (CANNOT MIGRATE - requires duration tracking)
DEFER_TEMPORAL.reason: Temporal mechanics require duration/round tracking, not state predicates
examples: Effect ends at beginning of next round

DEFER_UNKNOWN.category: Abilities with unclear or complex mechanics requiring manual audit
instructions: 3. If yes: assign predicates and migrate to PASSIVE/STATE
4. If no: decide whether RULE, TRIGGERED, or stays DEFERRED
5. Document decision in this file under reviewed_*
